campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's financial records from July 2025, including how the city's money was invested and spent that month. They also discussed plans for improving transportation options around Campbell, looking at ways to help people get around using different methods like buses, bikes, and walking.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment and spending report
City treasurer's monthly financial update
Campbell Multimodal Transportation Plan (improving transit, biking, and pedestrian options)

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a public hearing to get community input on a new rule or policy. After listening to residents, the council decided to move forward with the proposal and will vote to officially approve it at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
New ordinance introduced (specific topic not detailed in agenda)
Vote scheduled for next regular council meeting
First reading of ordinance waived to speed up approval process

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, primarily to approve meeting minutes from various sessions held in March. The council also authorized the Town Manager to move forward with a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the full details of this project were cut off in the agenda provided.
keyTopics: Approval of March meeting minutes from multiple sessions
Construction contract authorization with QLM, Inc.
Town Manager given authority to execute construction agreement

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 15, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize and finalize their upcoming agendas for late April 2026. They reviewed what items should be added to their April 21st and April 28th meetings, decided which topics needed specific time slots, and checked on any documents that needed special approval to discuss.
keyTopics: Finalized the April 21, 2026 council meeting agenda
Planned items for the April 28, 2026 draft agenda
Set specific time slots for important agenda items
Reviewed public records from April 2-9, 2026
VEBA Advisory Committee discussion

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting where they reviewed the city's spending plans for the next fiscal year, including money earmarked for building and infrastructure projects. The council also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and community volunteers in Mountain View.
keyTopics: 2026-27 budget for city construction and improvement projects
Teen Month recognition and proclamation
National Volunteer Week acknowledgment

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 14, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review notes from their previous meeting and discuss changes to tenant protection rules in Sunnyvale. The main focus was introducing new amendments to the city's residential tenant protections program, which affects the rights and responsibilities of renters and landlords.
keyTopics: Approval of March 25, 2026 meeting minutes
New amendments to tenant protection rules (Chapter 19.71 of city code)
Residential tenant protections program updates
Housing and Human Services Commission business

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 13, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a workshop to help residents learn about OpenGov, a tool that lets you see how the city spends your tax dollars. The council also discussed new rules requiring people who lobby the city to register and report what issues they're trying to influence.
keyTopics: OpenGov financial transparency tool demo for public use
Lobbyist registration and reporting requirements under city ordinance
Community engagement on city budget spending
New rules for those influencing city legislative decisions

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 15, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a Charter Review Committee meeting on April 15, 2026. Council Chair Pat Nikolai joined the meeting remotely from Oregon. The city offered residents multiple ways to participate, including in-person attendance, Zoom video call, or phone call.
keyTopics: Charter Review Committee meetings continuing in Santa Clara
Chair Nikolai participating remotely from Curtin, Oregon
Multiple participation options: in-person, Zoom, and phone
Written public comments accepted via email to cityattorney@santaclaraca.gov by 10 AM

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to review and approve several procedural items, including updates to the city's long-term housing and community development plan for 2025-2030. They also discussed potential changes to how the city fills council vacancies and reviewed requests from individual council members for new items to work on in the coming year.
keyTopics: Updates to the 2025-2030 housing and community development plan
Proposed change to the council vacancy filling process
New workplan requests from council members
Approval of previous meeting minutes and upcoming meeting schedule

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a public hearing about a building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown that the owner wants to upgrade from a lower to higher historic status. If approved, this upgrade would allow the owner to add 5,000 square feet of new floor space as a reward for preserving and seismically strengthening the historic building.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic designation upgrade request
5,000 sq ft bonus floor area for historic and seismic improvements
Downtown Commercial zone development
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule and assignments
March 12, 2026 meeting minutes approval
